_comment: Declarative transformation rules for the Silver layer. The Python asset reads these rules to stay generic: column renames, country/year scaffold expansion, and enrichment key definitions all live here.

default._comment: Base transformation contract applied to every dataset before dataset-specific overrides.

countries._comment: The countries dataset is special: it becomes the reference scaffold for the warehouse grain and therefore expands to one row per country/year.

expand_year_range._comment: This range drives the country/year scaffold used later by Gold joins and by the final OBT.

gdp_per_capita_usd._comment: Rename the generic IMF `value` column into a semantic warehouse column name.

gdp_per_capita_ppp._comment: Same pattern as above: convert a generic input schema into an explicit analytical schema.

gdp_ppp_world_share._comment: The renamed field makes the metric self-descriptive once loaded into BigQuery and consumed by BI tools.

unemployment_rate._comment: Dataset-specific naming keeps downstream SQL readable and avoids ambiguous `value` columns.

public_debt_gdp._comment: The metric name embeds both its meaning and unit so the schema stays self-documenting.

inflation_avg_consumer._comment: Renaming happens in Silver so Gold and Looker consume explicit business column names only.

country_labels._comment: Lookup-driven enrichment that maps country codes to readable labels using the countries dataset as the authoritative source.

id_countryear._comment: Shared business key configuration. The Silver asset builds this key to guarantee a consistent join grain across all datasets.
